root_cause: Dissolved-gas analysis exceeds IEEE C57.104 condition 3 limits (H₂ > 700 ppm and/or C₂H₂ > 5 ppm). Acetylene above the 5 ppm threshold is the strongest single indicator of internal arcing in a mineral-oil transformer and should be treated as a presumptive incipient fault until laboratory analysis disproves it.

recommended_actions.action: Remove the asset from service or de-rate to ≤ 30% of nameplate kVA pending laboratory DGA confirmation.
recommended_actions.rationale: Internal arcing can escalate to bushing failure or tank rupture with little warning. Service removal is the conservative posture until ratios are confirmed.

recommended_actions.action: Dispatch a sampling crew within 8 hours and request expedited laboratory turnaround (target: 48 h).
recommended_actions.rationale: Fresh DGA + Duval triangle classification is the only diagnostic that distinguishes arcing in oil from arcing involving cellulose — the two have different repair paths.

recommended_actions.action: Notify control center and prepare alternate feeder routing in case full removal is required.
recommended_actions.rationale: If DGA confirms condition 3 with acetylene, removal from service is mandatory. Pre-staging the topology change minimizes customer-minutes lost.

recommended_actions.action: Schedule electrical tests (turns ratio, insulation resistance, sweep frequency response analysis) prior to any return-to-service decision.
recommended_actions.rationale: After an arcing fault, electrical-test signatures must be compared to factory or commissioning baselines before re-energization.
